text.title: GitHub Projects Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding GitHub Projects. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: One Project per outcome / initiative — not per repo. Per-repo Projects fragment cross-repo work.
rule.rationale: Projects v2 is designed for outcome tracking; per-repo defeats the design.

rule.statement: Projects link to ≥1 repo; issues auto-import when assigned to milestone / label.
rule.rationale: Without repo linkage, Projects becomes a wiki — slower than just spreadsheets.

rule.statement: ≤8 custom fields per Project. More = drift + unmaintained.
rule.rationale: Field bloat is the leading death cause of GitHub Project v2 boards.

rule.statement: Use ONE primary grouping axis: Iteration (sprint) OR Status (kanban). Mixing produces incoherent views.
rule.rationale: Two axes confuse the team about which view is authoritative.

rule.statement: Automation lives in GitHub Actions workflows (label-on-merge, close-on-merge), not in Projects UI rules. UI rules silently break across reorgs.
rule.rationale: Actions workflows are versioned + reviewable; Projects UI rules are not.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-github-projects.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
